prompt:
Database schema (SQLite):

Table Highschooler(ID, name, grade)
Table Friend(student_id, friend_id)
Table Likes(student_id, liked_id)

Question: Find the names of students who have at least 2 friends and also have at least 1 person they like. Return each qualifying student's name.

Database schema (SQLite):

Table employee(Employee_ID, Name, Age, City)
Table shop(Shop_ID, Name, Location, District, Number_products, Manager_name)
Table hiring(Shop_ID, Employee_ID, Start_year, Is_full_time)
Table evaluation(Employee_ID, Year_awarded, Bonus)

Question: Which shops employ more full-time workers than the average number of full-time workers per shop? Return shop name and full-time employee count.

difficulty_note: Hard — nested aggregation across multiple joins.
prompt:
Database schema (SQLite):

Table Students(student_id, first_name, last_name, email_address, date_first_registered, date_left, other_details)
Table Student_Enrolment(student_enrolment_id, student_id, semester_id, degree_program_id, other_details)
Table Student_Enrolment_Courses(student_course_id, course_id, student_enrolment_id)
Table Courses(course_id, course_name, course_description, other_details)
Table Semesters(semester_id, semester_name, semester_description, other_details)

Question: Find the first name and last name of the student who enrolled in the most courses. If there is a tie, return all tied students.
